posts.title: Venezuelan Podium at Silverstone: Strong GB4 Debut
posts.summary: A strong first GB4 weekend at Silverstone, with immediate front-running pace and a podium result.
posts.body:
## Versión en Español

Emmilio Valentino Del Grosso logró un resultado destacado en su fin de semana de debut en el GB4 Championship, consiguiendo un podio en el icónico Silverstone Circuit.

Con tan solo 16 años, Emmilio entró en uno de los campeonatos de monoplazas más competitivos con un objetivo claro: luchar en la parte delantera. Y lo demostró desde el inicio.

### Resumen de Carreras

**Carrera 1 — P2**  
Una actuación sólida y controlada que resultó en un segundo lugar, confirmando el ritmo y la ejecución frente a un nivel muy competitivo.

**Carrera 2 — DNF, problema técnico**  
Un inconveniente mecánico obligó a abandonar la carrera. A pesar de esto, el rendimiento general se mantuvo sólido.

**Carrera 3 — P5 desde P9**  
Una remontada efectiva y bien gestionada, ganando posiciones gracias a un ritmo constante, adelantamientos limpios y una estrategia inteligente.

### Puntos Clave

- Ritmo competitivo desde el debut
- Sólida gestión de carrera y consistencia
- Capacidad de recuperación bajo presión
- Posicionamiento competitivo dentro del grupo

Este resultado marca un paso importante y refuerza la progresión hacia actuaciones constantes en la parte delantera.

> “Esto demuestra que puedo luchar por victorias.”

El enfoque ahora está en mantener este impulso y apuntar al escalón más alto del podio.

## English Version

Emmilio Valentino Del Grosso delivered an impressive performance on his debut weekend in the GB4 Championship, securing a podium finish at the iconic Silverstone Circuit.

At just 16 years old, Emmilio entered one of the most competitive junior single-seater environments with a clear objective: to compete at the front. He delivered immediately.

### Race Summary

**Race 1 — P2**  
A strong and composed drive resulted in a second-place finish, confirming both pace and execution against a highly competitive field.

**Race 2 — DNF, technical issue**  
A mechanical problem forced an early retirement. Despite the setback, overall performance remained strong.

**Race 3 — P5 from P9**  
A controlled and effective recovery drive, gaining positions through consistent pace, clean overtakes and smart race management.

### Key Takeaways

- Immediate front-running pace on debut
- Strong racecraft and consistency
- Proven ability to recover under pressure
- Competitive positioning within the field

This result marks an important milestone and reinforces the trajectory towards consistent front-running performances.

> “This shows I can fight for wins.”

The focus now shifts to building on this momentum and targeting the top step of the podium.

posts.title: GB4: The Next Step
posts.summary: The move into GB4 with Elite Motorsport marks the next chapter in the single-seater journey.
posts.body:
I am excited to announce that I will be stepping up into the GB4 Championship for the 2026 season, contesting the series with Elite Motorsport.

This marks a significant milestone in my racing journey: my first full season in single-seaters after completing the 2025 campaign in the Ginetta Junior Championship. In that season I learned a huge amount, adapted quickly to car racing and showed consistent pace and improvement.

Joining the GB4 field is something I have been working toward for a long time. The level of competition is strong, and I am ready to embrace the challenge, push myself every weekend and keep developing as a driver.

I am grateful to remain within the Elite Motorsport family. Their support and belief in me has been a driver in my progress. This step up will be demanding, but I am focused, prepared and hungry to make an impact.

Stay tuned for more updates as we approach the first round of the season.

posts.title: Donington: The Final Fight
posts.summary: A strong way to close the season, with race pace, fightbacks and proof of progress under pressure.
posts.body:
The final round of the season took us to Donington Park. Practice went well, sitting comfortably inside the top ten and feeling confident with the car.

Qualifying was tricky. The track was drying, conditions kept changing and a few mistakes cost us. We ended up P17 for Race 1 and P15 for Race 2. Not ideal, but the race pace was there.

**Race 1** was one of the strongest drives of the year in terms of racecraft and consistency. It was hard, clean racing all the way through, fighting forward from P17 to P10.

**Race 2** started well and we climbed to P7, but another driver missed the braking point and took me out completely. The contact caused heavy damage and put the final race in doubt.

**Race 3** was impossible to start because of the damage from Race 2. It was not the way we wanted to end the weekend, but that is racing.

Overall, it was still a strong weekend and a positive way to close the season. We showed how much we evolved mentally, technically and as a team. I am proud of the progress and ready for what comes next.

posts.title: Croft: Hard Fights, Real Progress
posts.summary: A weekend of pace, resilience and wet-weather progress at Croft.
posts.body:
Croft was a strong weekend from the start. We had our best qualifying of the season, putting it P8 for both races and setting ourselves right in the mix with the front group.

**Race 1** started well. I moved up to P7, overtook the championship leader and began closing the gap to the leaders. Unfortunately, while defending, I was taken out and dropped back to P17.

From there it was full focus. I pushed hard, fought through the field and brought it home in P13. The pace was there, and the fightback showed it.

**Race 2** started clean with no places lost on lap one, but contact later in the race ended it early. Frustrating, but part of racing.

**Race 3** started from P20 in tricky wet conditions. I made a few mistakes early on, but stayed composed and kept pushing, climbing to P11 in one of my best drives of the season mentally and behind the wheel.

Overall, the weekend was about growth. We showed speed, resilience and progress. The result did not show everything, but the important thing was being able to stay with the front and fight.

posts.title: Brands Hatch: Step by Step Forward
posts.summary: A weekend of learning, strong practice pace and hard racing at Brands Hatch.
posts.body:
We went into the weekend full of confidence. Practice went great, ending P5 overall. The grid was tight and everyone was on the limit, so being that high up was a strong start.

Slipstream played a big role in qualifying again. I put together a clean lap, but without a tow it was difficult to match the front-runners. We qualified P15 for Race 1 and P13 for Race 2. Even then, the pace showed that with slipstream we could be much closer to the front.

Race 1 started with a mega launch off the line, straight into the top ten. The opening laps were intense. I fought hard but got caught in battles ahead, which allowed the chasing pack to close in. We finished P16, frustrating because the pace was there.

Race 2 was stopped after a big incident. There is not much to say apart from being glad everyone was alright.

Race 3 started P15 and finished P11. The race was messy, with red flags and interruptions, so we never fully got the chance to show our potential.

Overall, I learned a lot. Brands Hatch is one of those tracks I always dreamed of racing at, so finally doing it felt special. Despite the setbacks, the experience was incredible. We keep pushing.

posts.title: Grinding Through Zandvoort: A Weekend of Progress
posts.summary: Progress, composure and fightback pace through a tough Zandvoort weekend.
posts.body:
Coming into Zandvoort, things looked promising. Testing went great and we had strong pace from the start, finishing P4 in practice and running close with the championship contenders.

Qualifying was difficult because the top ten drivers all benefited from slipstream, putting us on the back foot. Despite that, we still managed P15 for Race 1 and P14 for Race 2.

Race 1 was intense from the start. Chaos on lap one dropped us down to P19, but the car felt good and we fought back through the field to P14. The result did not fully show the pace, but the fight was solid.

Race 2 started better. After lap one we were already up to P11, but an unfortunate collision sent us back to P16. I stayed focused and fought back again to finish P11.

Race 3 was hectic from lights out. A tough start cost a few places early, but I recovered and crossed the line P11 before the red flag ended the race early.

Overall, I was happy with the progress. Mentally, it was a strong weekend. I stayed calm through setbacks and kept fighting every lap. We showed pace, control and real improvement.
